diagnosis: First time the cistarget stage actually runs on real PBMC RNA/ATAC data with real aertslab hg38 v10 motif rankings — 35,410 enriched pairs in 1.5s.
Pipeline stalls in the eRegulon assembly step's `gene-only — bridging via GRN targets (approximate)` path.
When only `motif_rankings` (gene-based) is provided, pipeline.run bridges to peak-level via GRN ∩ enhancer links. The bridge does Python loops that are slow at 35k cistarget × 10k links scale.
Region-based cistarget path (`region_motif_rankings` argument, exact eRegulon assembly) was added in v0.3.0 and is the real fix for users; this gene-only bridge is a fallback.

Stages 1-5 are demonstrated working on real PBMC Multiome data with the real aertslab feather. Downstream enhancer/eRegulon biological interpretation still requires real gene TSS coordinates; synthetic/random TSS coordinates are structural smoke only.
next_step: Profile + speed up the gene-only bridge in pipeline.run (eRegulon assembly section). Or: cleaner — recommend `region_motif_rankings` for real workflows in the docs.
